calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn On Petition Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
George Surmaitis, Esq. of Leung & Associates, 415-933-9988 for plaintiffs and moving parties WILLIAM PALLAS, GERSON GARCIA, DANIEL MCGUFFIN, ROBERT CONRAD, SALVADOR DEL REAL
Wayne Snodgrass, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4675 for defendant and opposing party San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency

Ruling:
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
PLAINTIFF WILLIAM PALLAS, GERSON GARCIA, DANIEL MCGUFFIN, ROBERT CONRAD, SALVADOR DEL REAL'S Notice Of Motion On Petition Of Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. EXISTING LAW CONTROLS THE APPLICATION OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. DEFENDANT CAN PRESENTLY HOLD MEDALLIONS FOR SALE AND PLAINTIFFS DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY DISTINGUISH CITY OF WATSONVILLE V. STATE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES (2005) 133 CAL APP 4TH 875, PROFESSIONAL ENGINEERS IN CALIF. GOVT. V. SCHWARZENEGGER (2010) 50 CAL 4TH 989, AND MARINE FORESTS SOCIETY V. CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMM. (2005) 36 CAL 4TH 1. THE BOARD ULTIMATELY RATIFIED DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SUFFCIENTLY DEMONSTRATE A MANDATORY DUTY. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797 (302/CWW)
